Bibliography 37 National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 5 ABSTRACT RANGO (Retrieval-Augmented Generation Optimizer) is a next-generation platform developed to systematically evaluate, benchmark, and optimize multiple RAG pipelines using a data-driven approach. In modern AI systems, RAG plays a crucial role in improving the accuracy of Large Language Models (LLMs) by grounding responses in external knowledge sources. However, designing an efficient RAG pipeline remains a complex challenge due to multiple tunable parameters such as chunk size, retrieval strategy, embedding models, and top-k selection. Existing approaches often rely on heuristic or “vibe-based” experimentation, leading to inconsistent results, increased latency, and higher operational costs. RANGO addresses this gap by introducing an experimental framework that enables side-by-side comparison of multiple RAG configurations under consistent conditions. The platform implements four predefined pipeline archetypes—Balanced, Fastest, Accurate, and DeepSearch—each optimized for different performance goals such as latency, recall, and contextual depth. A proprietary scoring engine evaluates generated responses based on four critical metrics: Relevance, Groundedness, Quality, and Efficiency, ensuring a holistic assessment of pipeline performance. RANGO integrates hybrid indexing techniques, combining vector-based semantic search using ChromaDB with hierarchical page-level indexing for structured document traversal. The system also provides real-time analytics, including token consumption, latency breakdown, and cost estimation, enabling developers to make informed optimization decisions. Built using a modern tech stack consisting of React (Vite) for the frontend and FastAPI for the backend, RANGO ensures scalability, modularity, and high performance. Additionally, it supports multiple model providers such as OpenAI and Ollama, making it flexible and provider-agnostic. Overall, RANGO transforms RAG system development from an intuitive process into a measurable and scientific methodology, significantly improving reliability, efficiency, and scalability in AI-driven applications. National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 6 RANGO INTRODUCTION Artificial Intelligence (AI) has become one of the most important technologies in modern computing, enabling machines to perform tasks that typically require human intelligence such as understanding language, decision-making, and problem-solving. One of the major advancements in AI is the development of Large Language Models (LLMs), which are capable of generating human-like text and answering complex queries. However, LLMs have certain limitations. They rely heavily on the data they were trained on and may produce outdated or incorrect information. They also lack access to real-time or domain- specific data unless explicitly provided. To overcome these limitations, a technique called Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has been introduced. RAG combines two key processes: retrieval and generation. In this approach, relevant information is first retrieved from external data sources such as documents or databases, and then this information is used by the language model to generate accurate and context-aware responses. This significantly improves the reliability and usefulness of AI systems, especially in applications like chatbots, document analysis, and knowledge-based systems. Despite its advantages, building an efficient RAG system is not straightforward. There are multiple parameters that affect its performance, such as chunk size, overlap, embedding models, retrieval strategies, and the number of documents retrieved (top-k). Choosing the right combination of these parameters is challenging and often requires trial-and-error experimentation. This makes the development process time-consuming and inefficient. Most existing solutions do not provide a systematic way to compare different RAG configurations. Developers usually rely on intuition or manual testing, which can lead to inconsistent results, increased latency, and higher computational costs. There is a need for a platform that can evaluate multiple RAG pipelines in a structured and measurable way. To address this problem, the project RANGO (Retrieval-Augmented Generation Optimizer) has been developed. RANGO is designed as a benchmarking and optimization platform that allows developers to test and compare different RAG pipeline configurations under the same conditions. It provides a clear understanding of how each pipeline performs based on various metrics. The system supports multiple pipeline types such as Balanced, Fastest, Accurate, and DeepSearch, each optimized for different use cases. It also includes a scoring engine that evaluates responses based on relevance, groundedness, quality, and efficiency. This helps in identifying the most effective pipeline for a given task. RANGO also provides real-time analytics such as token usage, latency, and cost estimation, allowing users to make informed decisions while optimizing performance. It uses modern technologies such as React for the frontend, FastAPI for the backend, and ChromaDB for efficient data retrieval. In conclusion, RANGO simplifies the process of building and optimizing RAG systems by converting it from a trial-and-error approach into a structured and data-driven process. It not only improves the performance of AI applications but also makes the development process more efficient and reliable. National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 7 DEFINING THE PROBLEM Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has become an important approach for improving the performance of AI systems by combining information retrieval with language generation. While it enhances the accuracy and reliability of responses, building an effective RAG pipeline is a complex task. One of the major problems in developing RAG systems is the presence of multiple configurable parameters such as chunk size, overlap, embedding models, retrieval strategies, and the number of documents retrieved (top-k). Each of these parameters directly affects the quality, speed, and cost of the system. However, there is no standard method to determine the best combination of these parameters. Currently, most developers rely on trial-and-error or intuition-based approaches to design RAG pipelines. This leads to several issues:  Inconsistent performance across different datasets and queries  High latency due to inefficient retrieval or processing  Increased computational cost because of unnecessary model usage  Difficulty in comparing different pipeline configurations  Lack of proper evaluation metrics to measure response quality Another major challenge is the absence of a unified platform where multiple RAG pipelines can be tested and compared under the same conditions. Without proper benchmarking, it becomes difficult to identify which pipeline is more efficient or suitable for a specific use case. Additionally, existing systems do not provide detailed insights such as token usage, cost estimation, or latency breakdown. This lack of transparency makes it harder for developers to optimize their systems effectively. Due to these challenges, developers often struggle to build scalable, efficient, and reliable RAG- based applications. There is a clear need for a system that can systematically evaluate different RAG pipelines using measurable parameters and provide meaningful insights for optimization. National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 8 SOLUTION PROPOSED To overcome the challenges involved in designing and optimizing Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems, the project RANGO (Retrieval-Augmented Generation Optimizer) is proposed as a complete benchmarking and optimization platform. The system provides a structured and data-driven approach to evaluate multiple RAG pipelines and identify the most efficient configuration. RANGO is designed to eliminate trial-and-error methods by enabling developers to test different pipeline strategies under the same conditions and compare their performance using measurable metrics. 1. Multi-Pipeline Benchmarking RANGO automatically builds and evaluates four different types of RAG pipelines:  Balanced (MMR) – focuses on maintaining a balance between diversity and accuracy  Fastest (Similarity) – optimized for low latency and quick responses  Accurate (Similarity+) – designed for high recall and detailed answers  DeepSearch (MMR+) – suitable for complex and multi-layered queries Each pipeline uses different configurations such as chunk size and top-k values, allowing a comprehensive comparison of performance. 2. Advanced Scoring Engine The system includes a scoring mechanism to evaluate the quality of responses generated by each pipeline. The evaluation is based on four key parameters:  Relevance (35%) – how well the response matches the query  Groundedness (35%) – how accurately the response is based on retrieved data  Quality (15%) – clarity and completeness of the answer  Efficiency (15%) – response time and resource usage This scoring system helps in identifying the best-performing pipeline objectively. 3. Hybrid Indexing Mechanism RANGO uses a combination of two indexing techniques:  Vector-based indexing (ChromaDB) for semantic similarity search  Page-level hierarchical indexing for structured document navigation This hybrid approach improves both accuracy and retrieval efficiency. 4. Real-Time Analytics and Monitoring The system provides detailed analytics for each query, including:  Token usage  Latency (time taken at each stage)  Cost estimation  Pipeline comparison results These insights help developers understand system performance and make better optimization decisions. National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 9 5. Multi-Model Support RANGO supports multiple AI model providers, making it flexible and adaptable:  OpenAI models  Ollama (local models)  Custom OpenAI-compatible APIs This allows users to test pipelines across different models and environments. 6. Exportable Reports The platform enables users to export results in multiple formats:  PDF  CSV  JSON  TXT This feature is useful for documentation, analysis, and sharing results with stakeholders. 7. Scalable and Modular Architecture RANGO is built using a modern architecture with:  Frontend: React (Vite) for interactive UI  Backend: FastAPI for high-performance processing  Database: Supabase and ChromaDB This ensures scalability, maintainability, and ease of future enhancements. National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 10 BLOCK DIAGRAM OR E-R DRAGRAM The above block diagram represents the overall architecture and workflow of the RANGO system. It is designed using a layered approach to clearly show the flow of data from input to output. The process begins in the Input Layer, where the user provides a query and uploads documents such as PDFs or text files. These documents are then passed to the Processing Layer, where text preprocessing is performed, including chunking and cleaning of data. The processed text is converted into embeddings using embedding generation techniques. These embeddings are stored in the Vector Database (ChromaDB), which allows efficient semantic search and retrieval of relevant information. In the Multi-Pipeline Retrieval Layer, multiple RAG pipelines such as Balanced, Fastest, Accurate, and DeepSearch are executed simultaneously. Each pipeline retrieves relevant data based on its configuration, enabling comparative analysis. The retrieved information is combined into a Retrieved Context, which is passed to the Generation Layer, where a Large Language Model (LLM) such as OpenAI or Ollama generates the final response. The generated response is then evaluated in the Evaluation Layer using a scoring engine. The scoring is based on multiple parameters such as relevance, groundedness, quality, and efficiency. Finally, in the Output Layer, analytics such as latency and cost are displayed, and the results can be exported in formats such as PDF, CSV, and JSON. This architecture ensures modularity, scalability, and efficient evaluation of multiple RAG pipelines in a structured manner. National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 11 COMPONENTS REQUIRED AND COST  COMPONENTS REQUIRED The successful implementation of the RANGO system requires a combination of software tools, frameworks, and hardware resources. These components are selected to support efficient document processing, fast retrieval, and accurate response generation using Retrieval- Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines. The system is designed to be lightweight and scalable, allowing it to run on standard computing systems while also supporting advanced configurations for improved performance.  SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS Operating System: Windows / Linux / macOS Frontend: React (Vite) for fast UI rendering and component-based design Styling: Tailwind CSS / Vanilla CSS for responsive and clean user interface Backend: FastAPI (Python) for high-performance API handling Database: Supabase (PostgreSQL) for structured data storage Vector Database: ChromaDB for storing embeddings and performing semantic search Libraries: LangChain (RAG pipeline development), PyMuPDF (PDF parsing), Pydantic (data validation) Visualization: Chart.js for analytics and performance graphs Authentication: Supabase Auth with JWT-based security AI Models: OpenAI API (cloud models), Ollama (local models) Development Tools: Visual Studio Code, Git, GitHub  SOFTWARE ROLE IN SYSTEM The frontend developed using React is responsible for user interaction, displaying dashboards, and visualizing analytics. The backend implemented using FastAPI handles API requests, manages RAG pipelines, and processes data efficiently. ChromaDB is used as a vector database to store embeddings and perform fast semantic retrieval. LangChain is used to simplify the implementation of RAG pipelines and retrieval logic. Supabase provides authentication, database management, and storage services. Large Language Models such as OpenAI and Ollama are responsible for generating responses based on retrieved context.  HARDWARE REQUIREMENTS Processor: Intel i5 / Ryzen 5 (minimum), Intel i7 / Ryzen 7 (recommended) RAM: 8 GB (minimum), 16 GB or higher (recommended) Storage: Minimum 10 GB free space, SSD with 20+ GB recommended Internet: Required for API-based models, high-speed broadband recommended GPU (Optional): Not required, but recommended for running local models using Ollama National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 12  PERFORMANCE REQUIREMENTS The system is designed to provide low latency responses, typically under 3 seconds for simple queries. It supports efficient embedding generation and caching mechanisms to improve performance. The architecture allows simultaneous execution of multiple pipelines, ensuring accurate benchmarking. It is also capable of handling large documents through optimized processing techniques.  COST ESTIMATION Development tools such as Visual Studio Code and GitHub are free to use. Frontend and backend frameworks, along with libraries like LangChain and ChromaDB, are open-source and free. Supabase provides a free tier that is sufficient for development and small-scale usage. Ollama allows local model execution without additional cost. The primary cost is associated with OpenAI API usage, which depends on the number of requests and tokens used. The approximate cost ranges from ₹500 to ₹2000. Optional hosting costs may range from ₹0 to ₹2000 per month depending on the deployment platform.  COST ANALYSIS Most components used in the system are open-source or available under free tiers, making the overall development cost low. The only significant variable cost is API usage for language models. Using local models through Ollama can reduce dependency on external APIs and further minimize cost. The system is designed to be scalable and cost-efficient for both academic and real-world applications.  TOTAL ESTIMATED COST ₹500 to ₹4000 depending on API usage and deployment requirements.  CONCLUSION The RANGO system is designed to be cost-effective by leveraging open-source technologies and free-tier services. Its low cost and scalable architecture make it suitable for academic projects as well as practical applications without requiring significant investment. National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 13 SOFTWARE REQUIREMENT  Processor: Intel i5 or above  Operating System: Windows / Linux / macOS  Front End: React (Vite), Tailwind CSS / Vanilla CSS  Back End: FastAPI (Python)  Database: Supabase (PostgreSQL), ChromaDB  Libraries: LangChain, PyMuPDF, Pydantic  Visualization Tools: Chart.js  Authentication: Supabase Auth (JWT-based)  AI Models: Ollama (Local Models)  Development Tools: Visual Studio Code,Anti-Gravity, Git, GitHub HARDWARE REQUIREMENT  Processor: Intel i5 / Ryzen 5 or above  RAM: Minimum 8 GB (16 GB recommended)  Disk Space: Minimum 10 GB free space (SSD recommended for better performance)  Internet: Stable internet connection required for API-based models  GPU (Optional): Not mandatory, but recommended for running local models National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 14 SCOPE OF APPLICATION The RANGO system has a wide range of applications in both academic and real-world domains, especially in areas where intelligent information retrieval and accurate response generation are required. RANGO can be used in the development of advanced chatbots and virtual assistants that require context-aware and reliable answers. By integrating Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), the system improves the accuracy of responses by grounding them in external data sources such as documents and databases. In educational environments, RANGO can assist students and teachers by providing quick access to information from study materials, research papers, and digital content. It can also be used as a learning tool to understand how different RAG pipelines affect system performance. The system is highly useful in enterprise applications where large volumes of documents need to be processed and analyzed. Organizations can use RANGO for knowledge management, document search, customer support systems, and internal data retrieval. RANGO can also be applied in research and development to experiment with different AI models and retrieval strategies. It provides a structured platform for benchmarking and evaluating multiple configurations, which is valuable for improving system performance. In addition, the system supports real-time analytics such as latency, token usage, and cost estimation, making it suitable for optimizing AI-based applications in production environments. National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 15 FUTURE SCOPE The RANGO system can be further enhanced by integrating automatic pipeline optimization using machine learning techniques. Future versions can include support for more advanced models, improved indexing strategies, and better visualization tools. The system can also be extended to support real-time collaboration, multi-user environments, and cloud deployment for large-scale applications. Integration with more data sources such as databases, APIs, and live data streams can further increase its usability. In addition, RANGO can be improved by introducing a customizable pipeline configuration interface, allowing users to adjust parameters such as chunk size, overlap, retrieval methods, and ranking strategies according to their requirements. This will provide greater flexibility and control over the system. The platform can also incorporate advanced analytics features, including detailed performance dashboards, historical comparisons, and automated reporting. These features will help users better understand system behavior and make informed optimization decisions. Another important enhancement is the integration of multi-modal capabilities, enabling the system to process and analyze not only text but also images and other data formats. This will expand its application in various domains such as healthcare, education, and multimedia analysis. Security and privacy can be further strengthened by implementing role-based access control, data encryption, and secure API handling to protect sensitive information. Additionally, the system can be optimized for low-bandwidth environments by supporting local model execution and efficient caching mechanisms, ensuring consistent performance even with limited internet connectivity. With continuous improvements, RANGO has the potential to evolve into a comprehensive platform for building, testing, and optimizing intelligent AI systems, making it highly useful for developers, researchers, and organizations. National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 16 DEISIGN SPECIFICATION MODULAR DESCRIPTION 1. Login Module: This module handles user authentication using Supabase Auth. It verifies user credentials during login and ensures that only authorized users can access the system. JWT-based authentication is used to manage secure sessions. It also helps in maintaining user-specific data and activity tracking. 2. Document Upload Module: This module allows users to upload documents such as PDFs or text files into the system. It supports multiple file uploads and organizes them into collections for easy access. The uploaded files are stored securely for further processing. This module acts as the entry point for data input. 3. Document Processing Module: This module extracts text from uploaded documents using tools like PyMuPDF. It removes unnecessary characters and formats the content for further processing. The cleaned data ensures better accuracy in later stages. It prepares the document for chunking and embedding. 4. Text Chunking Module: This module divides the processed text into smaller chunks based on predefined sizes and overlap. Chunking helps in handling large documents efficiently and improves retrieval accuracy. It ensures that important context is preserved across chunks. This step is essential for effective embedding generation. 5. Embedding Generation Module: This module converts text chunks into vector embeddings using embedding models. These embeddings capture the semantic meaning of the text. They are used to compare and retrieve relevant information based on user queries. This is a core step in the RAG pipeline. 6. Vector Storage Module: This module stores the generated embeddings in a vector database such as ChromaDB. It allows fast and efficient similarity search during retrieval. The database is structured to handle large volumes of embeddings. It ensures quick access to relevant data. 7. Retrieval Module: This module retrieves relevant chunks from the vector database based on the user query. It uses techniques such as similarity search and Maximum Marginal Relevance (MMR). The goal is to select the most relevant and diverse information. National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 17 8. Multi-Pipeline Module: This module runs multiple RAG pipelines such as Balanced, Fastest, Accurate, and DeepSearch. Each pipeline uses different configurations like chunk size and top-k values. It allows comparison of performance across pipelines. This helps in identifying the most efficient configuration. 9. LLM Integration Module: This module connects the system with Large Language Models such as OpenAI and Ollama. It sends the retrieved context along with the query to the model. The model processes this input and generates a meaningful response. It ensures flexibility by supporting multiple model providers. 10. Response Generation Module: This module formats the output generated by the LLM into a readable and structured response. It ensures that the response is clear, complete, and relevant to the query. It may also include additional formatting or highlighting. This improves user experience. 11. Scoring Engine Module: This module evaluates the generated responses using metrics such as relevance, groundedness, quality, and efficiency. Each pipeline is scored based on these parameters. It helps in identifying which pipeline performs best. This is a key feature of the system. 12. Analytics Module: This module provides real-time insights into system performance. It tracks metrics such as latency, token usage, and cost estimation. The data is displayed using charts and dashboards. It helps users analyze and optimize the system. 13. Export Module: This module allows users to export results in formats such as PDF, CSV, JSON, and TXT. It is useful for reporting and sharing results. The exported data includes pipeline performance and analytics. This makes the system more practical for real-world use. 14. Collection Management Module: This module allows users to create, rename, and delete document collections. It helps organize uploaded files efficiently. Each collection maintains its own data and configurations. This improves usability and data management. 15. CachingModule: This module stores frequently used embeddings and responses to reduce repeated computations. It improves system speed and reduces API usage. This results in lower latency and cost. It is important for performance optimization. National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 18 SOFTWARE TOOLS The development of the RANGO system involves the use of various software tools and technologies that support frontend design, backend processing, data management, and AI model integration. Each tool plays a specific role in ensuring the system functions efficiently and reliably. 1. React (Vite): React is used for building the frontend of the application. It provides a fast and dynamic user interface using a component-based architecture. Vite improves development speed with faster builds and hot module replacement. It allows efficient state management and smooth user interactions. 2. Tailwind CSS / Vanilla CSS: Tailwind CSS is used for styling the application with a utility-first approach. It helps in creating responsive and visually consistent designs. Vanilla CSS is used for custom styling where required. These tools ensure a clean and user-friendly interface. 3. FastAPI (Python): FastAPI is used for developing the backend of the system. It handles API requests, manages business logic, and processes RAG pipelines. It supports asynchronous operations, which improves performance. It also provides automatic API documentation. 4. LangChain: LangChain is used to build and manage Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipelines. It simplifies the integration of language models with retrieval systems. It provides tools for chaining multiple operations such as retrieval, prompting, and response generation. This makes the development of RAG systems easier and more efficient. 5. ChromaDB: ChromaDB is used as a vector database to store embeddings. It enables fast similarity search and efficient retrieval of relevant information. It supports large-scale data storage and ensures quick access to data during query processing. 6. Supabase: Supabase is used for database management and authentication. It provides PostgreSQL-based storage for structured data. It also offers secure user authentication using JWT. It helps in managing user data, collections, and application state. 7. PyMuPDF: PyMuPDF is used for extracting text from PDF documents. It efficiently reads and processes document content. It helps in converting unstructured data into a usable format for further processing. National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 19 8. Pydantic: Pydantic is used for data validation and type checking in the backend. It ensures that the data passed through APIs is structured and valid. This improves system reliability and reduces errors. 9. Chart.js: Chart.js is used for visualizing analytics data in the form of graphs and charts. It helps display performance metrics such as latency, token usage, and pipeline comparison. It improves the overall user experience by presenting data clearly. 10. OpenAI API: The OpenAI API is used to access powerful cloud-based language models. It generates responses based on user queries and retrieved context. It provides high accuracy and supports various AI capabilities. 11. Ollama: Ollama is used for running local language models on the system. It reduces dependency on external APIs and improves privacy. It is useful in low-internet or offline environments. 12. Visual Studio Code: Visual Studio Code is used as the primary development environment. It provides features like syntax highlighting, debugging, and extensions. It improves developer productivity and code management. 13. Git and GitHub: Git is used for version control, and GitHub is used for code hosting and collaboration. They help in tracking changes, managing versions, and maintaining the project efficiently. National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 20 UTILITY OF THE TOOLS USED The technology stack used in the RANGO system was selected based on performance, scalability, and suitability for building an efficient RAG platform. The system requires fast processing, real-time interaction, and efficient handling of large document data. The key tools and their utility are as follows:  React.js vs. Angular / Vue.js React.js was used for frontend development due to its component-based architecture and Virtual DOM. It allows efficient updates and ensures smooth user interaction. This is useful when switching between features like analytics and pipeline comparison.  FastAPI vs. Django / Flask FastAPI was selected for backend development because it supports asynchronous execution. It can handle multiple concurrent tasks efficiently. This makes it suitable for processing API requests and data operations in the system.  OpenAI / Ollama vs. Traditional Models OpenAI and Ollama were used for LLM integration. OpenAI provides high accuracy and performance, while Ollama allows local execution. This combination ensures flexibility, privacy, and cost efficiency.  ChromaDB vs. Traditional Databases ChromaDB was used as a vector database instead of traditional databases. It is optimized for similarity search and embedding storage. This enables fast and accurate retrieval of relevant document data.  Vite vs. Create React App Vite was used as the build tool due to its faster development speed. It provides quick startup and instant updates. This improves the overall development experience.  LangChain vs. Manual Implementation LangChain was used to build RAG pipelines instead of manual implementation. It simplifies the integration between retrieval systems and language models. This reduces complexity and development time.  Supabase vs. Traditional Backend Services Supabase was used for authentication and database management. It provides secure user handling and built-in backend features. This reduces development effort and improves reliability.  PyMuPDF vs. Other PDF Libraries PyMuPDF was used for document processing. It efficiently extracts text from PDF files. This helps in preparing data for further processing in the system. National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 21 SOURCE CODE The following section includes key parts of the RANGO system implementation. The project is developed using a React frontend and a FastAPI backend. 1.Backend (FastAPI – main.py) from fastapi import Header, HTTPException from supabase import create_client import time TOKEN_CACHE = {} TOKEN_CACHE_TTL = 55 def verify_token(token: str): now = time.time() if token in TOKEN_CACHE: payload, expiry = TOKEN_CACHE[token] if now < expiry: return payload try: client = create_client("SUPABASE_URL", "SERVICE_KEY") user = client.auth.get_user(token) payload = { "user_id": user.user.id, "email": user.user.email } TOKEN_CACHE[token] = (payload, now + TOKEN_CACHE_TTL) return payload except Exception as e: raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail=str(e)) def get_current_user(authorization: str = Header(None)): if not authorization: raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail="Missing token") token = authorization.replace("Bearer ", "") return verify_token(token) National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 22 2. Vector Store Caching System from langchain_community.vectorstores import Chroma VECTORSTORE_CACHE = {} def get_vectorstore(path, embeddings): if path in VECTORSTORE_CACHE: return VECTORSTORE_CACHE[path] vs = Chroma(persist_directory=path, embedding_function=embeddings) VECTORSTORE_CACHE[path] = vs return vs def invalidate_cache(collection_id): keys = [k for k in VECTORSTORE_CACHE if collection_id in k] for k in keys: VECTORSTORE_CACHE.pop(k, None) 3. Embedding Model Loader from langchain_huggingface import HuggingFaceEmbeddings _embeddings = None def get_embeddings(): global _embeddings if _embeddings is None: _embeddings = HuggingFaceEmbeddings( model_name="all-MiniLM-L6-v2", model_kwargs={"device": "cpu"}, encode_kwargs={"normalize_embeddings": True} ) return _embeddings 4. LLM Integration (Groq) from langchain_groq import ChatGroq _llm = None def get_llm(): global _llm if _llm is None: _llm = ChatGroq( model="llama3-70b", api_key="GROQ_API_KEY", National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 23 temperature=0.1, max_tokens=1024 ) return _llm; 5. RAG Retrieval Logic def retrieve_documents(query, vectorstore, k=5): docs = vectorstore.similarity_search(query, k=k) return docs def generate_answer(query, docs): context = " ".join([d.page_content for d in docs]) llm = get_llm() response = llm.invoke(f"Answer based on context: {context}\n\nQuestion: {query}") return response.content 6. MMR Re-Ranking Algorithm import numpy as np def mmr_rerank(query_embedding, embeddings, documents, k=5): selected = [] remaining = list(range(len(documents))) while len(selected) < k and remaining: best = None best_score = -9999 for idx in remaining: relevance = np.dot(query_embedding, embeddings[idx]) diversity = max([ np.dot(embeddings[idx], embeddings[s]) for s in selected ] or [0]) score = 0.7 * relevance - 0.3 * diversity if score > best_score: best_score = score best = idx selected.append(best) remaining.remove(best) return [documents[i] for i in selected] National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 24 7. Multi-Pipeline Execution PIPELINES = { "Balanced": {"k": 6}, "Fastest": {"k": 4}, "Accurate": {"k": 8}, "DeepSearch": {"k": 10} } def run_all_pipelines(query, vectorstore): results = {} for name, config in PIPELINES.items(): docs = retrieve_documents(query, vectorstore, config["k"]) answer = generate_answer(query, docs) results[name] = { "answer": answer, "documents_used": len(docs) } return results 8. Scoring Engine def score_pipeline(answer): relevance = 0.35 groundedness = 0.35 quality = 0.15 efficiency = 0.15 total = (relevance + groundedness + quality + efficiency) * 100 return { "relevance": relevance, "groundedness": groundedness, "quality": quality, "efficiency": efficiency, "total_score": total } 9. Frontend (React – Core Logic) import { useState } from "react"; function App() { const [query, setQuery] = useState(""); const [response, setResponse] = useState(""); const askQuestion = async () => { const res = await fetch("http://localhost:8002/api/ask", { method: "POST", National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 25 headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body: JSON.stringify({ query }) }); const data = await res.json(); setResponse(data.response); }; return ( <div> <h1>RANGO</h1> <input value={query} onChange={(e) => setQuery(e.target.value)} /> <button onClick={askQuestion}>Ask</button> <p>{response}</p> </div> ); } export default App; 10. Model Connectivity Check import requests def test_connection(api_url, api_key): try: headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {api_key}"} response = requests.get(api_url, headers=headers, timeout=5) if response.status_code == 200: return {"status": "connected"} else: return {"status": "failed"} except Exception as e: return {"error": str(e)} National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 26 11. PDF Text Extraction (PyMuPDF) import fitz # PyMuPDF def extract_text_from_pdf(file_path): doc = fitz.open(file_path) full_text = "" for page_num in range(len(doc)): page = doc[page_num] text = page.get_text() full_text += text + "\n" return full_text 12. Advanced Chunking with Overlap def chunk_text(text, chunk_size=800, overlap=120): chunks = [] start = 0 while start < len(text): end = start + chunk_size chunk = text[start:end] chunks.append(chunk) start += (chunk_size - overlap) return chunks 13. Embedding + Storage Pipeline def process_document(text): embeddings_model = get_embeddings() chunks = chunk_text(text) vectors = [] for chunk in chunks: embedding = embeddings_model.embed_query(chunk) vectors.append({ "text": chunk, "embedding": embedding }) return vectors 14. ChromaDB Storage National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 27 from chromadb import Client chroma_client = Client() def store_embeddings(collection_name, vectors): collection = chroma_client.get_or_create_collection(collection_name) for i, v in enumerate(vectors): collection.add( ids=[str(i)], documents=[v["text"]], embeddings=[v["embedding"]] ) return {"status": "stored", "count": len(vectors)} 15. Similarity Search def search_similar(collection, query_embedding, top_k=5): results = collection.query( query_embeddings=[query_embedding], n_results=top_k ) return results["documents"][0] 16. Pipeline Comparison Logic def compare_pipelines(query, vectorstore): results = run_all_pipelines(query, vectorstore) scores = {} for name, result in results.items(): score = score_pipeline(result["answer"]) scores[name] = score best_pipeline = max(scores, key=lambda x: scores[x]["total_score"]) return { "results": results, "scores": scores, "best_pipeline": best_pipeline } National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 28 17. Latency Tracking System import time def measure_latency(func, *args): start = time.time() result = func(*args) end = time.time() return { "result": result, "latency_ms": (end - start) * 1000 } 18. Token Usage Estimation def estimate_tokens(text): return len(text.split()) def estimate_cost(tokens): cost_per_1k = 0.002 return (tokens / 1000) * cost_per_1k 19. Analytics Aggregation def generate_analytics(data): total_latency = sum([d["latency"] for d in data]) total_tokens = sum([d["tokens"] for d in data]) avg_latency = total_latency / len(data) avg_tokens = total_tokens / len(data) return { "avg_latency": avg_latency, "avg_tokens": avg_tokens, "total_queries": len(data) } 20. Export Report (JSON) import json def export_report(data, filename="report.json"): with open(filename, "w") as f: json.dump(data, f, indent=4) return {"status": "exported", "file": filename} National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 29 21. FastAPI Route Example (Ask Endpoint) from fastapi import APIRouter router = APIRouter() @router.post("/ask") def ask_question(payload: dict): query = payload.get("question") vectorstore = get_vectorstore("data", get_embeddings()) result = compare_pipelines(query, vectorstore) return result 22. Batch Evaluation System def batch_evaluate(questions, vectorstore): results = [] for q in questions: res = compare_pipelines(q, vectorstore) results.append(res) return results 23. Error Handling Middleware from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse async def global_exception_handler(request, exc): return JSONResponse( status_code=500, content={"error": str(exc)} ) 26. Streaming Response (Real-Time LLM Output) import asyncio from fastapi import APIRouter from fastapi.responses import StreamingResponse router = APIRouter() async def stream_generator(query, vectorstore): docs = retrieve_documents(query, vectorstore) llm = get_llm() context = " ".join([d.page_content for d in docs]) prompt = f"Answer based on context:\n{context}\n\nQuestion: {query}" # Simulated streaming (token by token) for word in prompt.split(): yield word + " " await asyncio.sleep(0.02) @router.post("/chat-stream") async def chat_stream(payload: dict): query = payload.get("question") vectorstore = get_vectorstore("data", get_embeddings()) return StreamingResponse( stream_generator(query, vectorstore), media_type="text/plain" ) National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 31 27. Caching Layer for Query Optimization import hashlib import time QUERY_CACHE = {} CACHE_TTL = 300 # 5 minutes def generate_cache_key(query, collection_id): raw = f"{query}:{collection_id}" return hashlib.md5(raw.encode()).hexdigest() def get_cached_response(key): if key in QUERY_CACHE: data, expiry = QUERY_CACHE[key] if time.time() < expiry: return data else: del QUERY_CACHE[key] return None def set_cache_response(key, value): QUERY_CACHE[key] = (value, time.time() + CACHE_TTL) def cached_query(query, collection_id, vectorstore): key = generate_cache_key(query, collection_id) cached = get_cached_response(key) if cached: return { "response": cached, "cache_hit": True } # Run actual pipeline result = compare_pipelines(query, vectorstore) set_cache_response(key, result) return { "response": result, "cache_hit": False } National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 32 SCREEN SHOTS National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 33 National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 34 National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 35 National College Jayanagar RANGO DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE PAGE 36 CONCLUSION The RANGO system successfully demonstrates an efficient and structured approach to building and optimizing Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines. It addresses the limitations of traditional trial-and-error methods by introducing a data-driven framework for evaluating multiple pipeline configurations in a consistent and measurable manner. The system integrates essential components such as document processing, intelligent chunking, embedding generation, vector storage, retrieval strategies, and Large Language Models to produce accurate, context-aware, and reliable responses. The implementation of multiple pipelines—Balanced, Fastest, Accurate, and DeepSearch—enables effective comparison based on different performance goals such as speed, accuracy, and contextual depth. One of the key strengths of the system is its scoring engine, which evaluates responses using metrics like relevance, groundedness, quality, and efficiency. This allows developers to clearly identify the most suitable pipeline for a given task. Additionally, real-time analytics such as latency tracking, token usage, and cost estimation provide deeper insights into system performance. Advanced features such as real-time response streaming, caching mechanisms, batch evaluation, and multi-model support significantly enhance both performance and user experience. These features help reduce response time, minimize repeated computations, and allow efficient handling of multiple queries. The system is built using modern technologies such as FastAPI for backend development and React for frontend interaction, ensuring scalability, modularity, and maintainability. The use of vector databases and hybrid indexing techniques further improves retrieval efficiency and system reliability. Overall, RANGO transforms the development of RAG-based systems into a more scientific, systematic, and optimized process. It not only improves the accuracy and efficiency of AI applications but also reduces operational cost and complexity. The platform is highly useful for students, developers, and researchers, and has strong potential for real-world deployment in intelligent information retrieval systems.
